full_text: The Singularity is distilling itself. OpenAI announced GPT-5.4 mini and nano , with mini leaping past GPT-5 mini across coding, reasoning, multimodal understanding, and tool use at 2x the speed, approaching the full 5.4 on benchmarks like SWE-Bench Pro and OSWorld-Verified. A trillion-parameter mystery model called Hunter Alpha surfaced on OpenRouter without attribution, fueling speculation that DeepSeek is stealth-testing its next generation in the wild. To measure what’s actually emerging, DeepMind released a Cognitive Taxonomy decomposing general intelligence into 10 faculties and launched a $200k Kaggle competition to close gaps in metacognition, attention, and social cognition. The models are already solving problems their creators can’t. HorizonMath , a benchmark of over 100 predominantly unsolved math problems, found GPT-5.4 Pro has already solved two open problems, proposing novel solutions that improve on best-known published results. Harmonic released Aristotle Agent , the world’s first autonomous mathematician, live and free. The DOE is aiming $293 million via the Genesis Mission at leveraging novel AI models to tackle over 20 national challenges in advanced manufacturing, biotech, critical materials, nuclear energy, and quantum science. Thanks for reading The Innermost Loop! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work. Biology is going from read-only archive to writable codebase. PerturbAI launched with the world’s largest in vivo CRISPR atlas, an 8-million-cell, brain-wide dataset capturing real biological circuitry in living tissue rather than isolated cell states. Xaira launched X-Cell , a new virtual cell model trained not on observational atlases but on 25.6 million perturbed cells, predicting gene expression changes across cell types, conditions, and unseen biology. Meanwhile, India-based PopVax argues that its generative AI, mRNA platforms, and 10x cheaper Indian R&D costs can make vaccines against neglected killers like HCV, TB, and Strep A economically viable to develop for the first time, saving millions of lives per year that pharma’s incentive model wrote off. Intelligence is worthless until it has a surface area. Anthropic shipped Dispatch in Claude Cowork, a persistent agent running on your computer that you can message from your phone, with observers noting Anthropic is building OpenClaw faster than OpenAI. Jensen Huang agreed on the destination, calling OpenClaw “definitely the next ChatGPT.” UCSD’s Dreamverse now generates 5-second 1080p clips in 4.55 seconds on a single GPU, faster than playback, turning video generation into real-time directing. Korean researchers announced the Seoul World Model , the first city-scale world simulation grounded in a real metropolis via RAG on millions of street-view images. Google is expanding “Personal Intelligence” across Search, Gemini, and Chrome. Microsoft is moving Mustafa Suleyman to first-party AI model development, cutting its dependence on third-party frontier labs. The superintelligence supply chain is a geopolitical chessboard. Nvidia won Beijing’s approval to sell H200 chips to China while prepping a Groq version for the same market. Infrastructure deals are turning litigious, with Microsoft weighing legal action against Amazon and OpenAI over a $50 billion cloud deal. Not everyone wants the compute next door. Rural Ohioans want to amend their state constitution to ban data centers above 25 megawatts. Jensen, undeterred, told GTC that Nvidia is engineering cooling for orbital data centers where convection doesn’t exist. War is already automated and publicly traded. Swarmer Inc. surged 700% at IPO, the best US debut since Newsmax, on the back of over 100,000 real-world combat drone missions in Ukraine since April 2024. The Pentagon is planning for AI to train on classified data , upgrading the national security stack from inference to learning. The economy is recalibrating around synthetic cognition. Gambling sites now accept bets on which jobs AI will replace . Companies are tracking individual employee token usage , scouting whose AI strategies deserve amplification and whose waste deserves pruning. In San Francisco’s newest gold rush, startups are competing for mansions as VC floods into multimillion-dollar hacker houses. USPS is auctioning its last-mile delivery for the first time, pushing Amazon to cut volume 60%. An appeals court temporarily unblocked Perplexity AI’s agentic shopping tool on Amazon, a win for synthetic commerce. The SEC issued a landmark token taxonomy carving out stablecoins, digital collectibles, and digital commodities as non-securities. Beyond the ledger, the cosmos is confirming that the ongoing intelligence explosion was likely inevitable. All five canonical nucleobases have now been found on asteroid Ryugu , strengthening the case that asteroids delivered the ingredients for life to Earth long ago. The building blocks arrived by asteroid, the weights by gradient descent. Thanks for reading The Innermost Loop! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.

description: The Singularity is no longer measured in breakthroughs but in procurement cycles.
truncated_text: The Singularity is no longer measured in breakthroughs but in procurement cycles. Jensen Huang predicted that Nvidia’s AI processors would generate $1 trillion in sales through 2027, and the company is building the coalition to prove it. The new Nemotron Coalition
full_text: The Singularity is no longer measured in breakthroughs but in procurement cycles. Jensen Huang predicted that Nvidia’s AI processors would generate $1 trillion in sales through 2027, and the company is building the coalition to prove it. The new Nemotron Coalition is providing DGX Cloud compute to partners including Mistral, Perplexity, Cursor, and Black Forest Labs, who are contributing data and expertise toward open models on Nvidia’s silicon. The models are just the beginning. NemoClaw wraps the OpenClaw agent platform with privacy and security guardrails, while DLSS 5 introduces a real-time neural rendering model that infuses game pixels with photoreal lighting, which Nvidia calls its biggest graphics breakthrough since ray tracing in 2018. The software stack needs new iron underneath it. New liquid-cooled Vera CPU racks pack 256 custom processors for reinforcement learning and agentic workloads that GPUs alone cannot handle, while the Groq 3 LPX rack pairs Nvidia’s licensed Groq inference chips with Vera Rubin for the second half of the year. And because Earth is only one customer, a dedicated Space-1 Vera Rubin Module will deliver 25x more AI compute for orbital data centers and autonomous space operations. Thanks for reading The Innermost Loop! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work. The chip alternatives are multiplying just as fast. Great Sky has launched a superconducting optoelectronic network that uses light to communicate data, claiming million-fold video processing speedups over conventional GPUs with less energy. Powering the new silicon requires new chemistry. Sodium-ion batteries hit the Midwestern grid in a first-of-its-kind pilot that cuts stored energy costs by roughly half, while in China, 100-kWh charging robots are reportedly turning every parking space into a mobile power bank. The data center is becoming civilization’s dominant building type. US construction spending on data centers surpassed offices for the first time in December, $3.57 billion to $3.49 billion, a shift that will accelerate as AI automates the jobs that once filled those offices. Meta is spending accordingly, committing up to $27 billion over five years to Nebius for dedicated AI infrastructure. OpenAI is pivoting Stargate from building data centers to renting cloud servers to accelerate deployment, while courting private equity firms including TPG, Advent, and Bain for a $10 billion enterprise AI venture designed to distribute its products across portfolio companies and offer a lifeline for holdings exposed to AI disruption. The velocity of the intelligence explosion is forcing even its leaders to triage. OpenAI’s top executives are refocusing the company around coding and business users , with CEO of applications Fidji Simo telling staff that Anthropic’s success should serve as a “wake-up call.” The urgency is justified: Codex has hit 2 million weekly active users , up nearly 4x since January, with API usage jumping 20% after GPT-5.4 launched. The company is also preparing an “adult mode” for ChatGPT enabling sexually explicit conversations, though it says it now “trains its models not to encourage exclusive relationships with users, and to remind users that they need to have relationships in the real world.” The scientific method is being compressed from a human ritual into a machine loop. ClawInstitute has launched as an open research exchange where AI agents publish hypotheses, ask questions, and ground claims in data. Google found that NotebookLM delivered surprisingly strong performance on research-level questions about high-temperature superconductors. And the THOR AI framework from UNM and Los Alamos used tensor networks to solve configurational integrals 400 times faster than advanced simulations. Biology is crossing its own threshold. J. Craig Venter and collaborators have demonstrated the first living synthetic bacterial cell by transplanting a complete genome into a dead cell, while Roche has deployed 3,500 Blackwell GPUs for biological foundation models and drug discovery. In a fitting irony, Beyond Meat is rebranding to “Beyond The Plant Protein Company” and expanding into protein drinks, moving beyond even its own name. The robots are learning to see through our eyes. Delivery bots are training on 30 billion images from Pokemon Go players , using crowdsourced views of buildings and landmarks to navigate where GPS falls short. The autonomous stack is consolidating around them, as BYD, Geely, Isuzu, and Nissan adopt Nvidia’s Drive Hyperion platform for Level 4 development. And the financial architecture is catching up to the tempo: the SEC is preparing to scrap quarterly earnings requirements in favor of semiannual reporting, cutting costs and discouraging short-termism the intelligence explosion has already made irrelevant. When the quarterly report dies, it’s because the machines already know. Thanks for reading The Innermost Loop! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.

description: The Singularity is writing its own source code.
truncated_text: The Singularity is writing its own source code. Some 70 to 90 percent of the code behind future Anthropic models is now written by Claude itself, and chief science officer Jared Kaplan believes fully automated AI research is less than a year away. Alignment lead Evan Hubinger is blunt: “Recursive self-improvement is not a future phenomenon. It is a pres…
full_text: The Singularity is writing its own source code. Some 70 to 90 percent of the code behind future Anthropic models is now written by Claude itself , and chief science officer Jared Kaplan believes fully automated AI research is less than a year away. Alignment lead Evan Hubinger is blunt: “Recursive self-improvement is not a future phenomenon. It is a present phenomenon.” Sam Altman is betting today’s frontier models can discover the next architecture after transformers. Percepta hard-coded a WebAssembly interpreter into transformer weights , executing arbitrary C code as tokens for millions of steps, proving neural nets are now practical, general-purpose computers. Anthropic is shipping 1M-token context windows for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6, stretching cognition to book length. Marc Andreessen thinks what emerges won’t be a paperclipper but “a goddess of compassion,” because intelligence at the highest levels looks like wisdom. New research argues the upper bound of conscious minds may be limited only by spacetime. If true, we are building a cathedral with no ceiling. The first open-source agentic AI physicist has arrived. Physical Superintelligence PBC launched Get Physics Done , a system that scopes problems, runs derivations, and verifies results against nature’s constraints. One early user called it the “best harness I have ever worked with.” Tom Kalil declared it was time to use Get Physics Done to map the civilizational tech tree at a granular level. Meanwhile, Terry Tao launched a Mathematics Distillation Challenge to compress reasoning into compact cheatsheets that boost LLM performance, turning system prompt optimization into a competitive sport. Thanks for reading The Innermost Loop! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work. The professions are capitulating faster than the machines can replace them. An AMA survey found 81 percent of physicians now use AI, more than double the 2023 rate. New US Senate guidelines permit aides to use Gemini, ChatGPT, and Copilot for official work. Ironically, in a world turned upside down by superintelligence, the Free Software Foundation is threatening to sue Anthropic for copyright infringement. The silicon supply chain is running a fever. Elon Musk revealed his “Terafab Project” launches this week, but QatarEnergy has not restarted helium production at Ras Laffan nine days after Iranian drone strikes knocked 30 percent of global supply offline, threatening the fabs that depend on ultrapure helium for cooling and leak detection. The AI-driven memory shortage is so absurd that RAM kits now ship with one fake stick alongside one real one, offering “desperate psychological relief.” The intelligence explosion is breaching containment into meatspace. Airbus is preparing two uncrewed combat aircraft from Kratos for first flight, targeting a German Air Force drone wingman by 2029. Uber co-founder Travis Kalanick relaunched his ghost kitchen company as “Atoms,” pivoting to “gainfully employed robots” for food, mining, and transport. The energy to power it all is arriving: Vineyard Wind , America’s first large-scale offshore wind farm, has finished construction. Physics itself is learning to whisper. Australian researchers demonstrated covert communications by modulating photon emissions above and below blackbody levels, achieving zero optical signature by hiding data in thermal noise. Biology is becoming transparent. Japanese researchers demonstrated isotonic optical clearing media for live imaging of organoids, brain slices, and mouse brains in vivo. In Texas, cultured meat startup Wildtype sued to overturn a Texas ban blocking cultivated salmon sales. A Sydney entrepreneur used ChatGPT to design a personalized mRNA cancer vaccine for his rescue dog, shrinking the tumor considerably. The financial architecture of the intelligence explosion is taking shape. SpaceX is dividing IPO roles among an unusually large bank syndicate, reflecting the offering’s scale. Meta is reportedly planning sweeping layoffs of 20 percent or more to offset AI costs. The old, new economy sheds staff while the new, new one files its S-1. We may not be the only civilization to have undergone an intelligence explosion. The White House’s planned UAP disclosure is reportedly “massive” according to former defense official Christopher Mellon, including satellite imagery of craft that look like nothing humans have built. Rep. Eric Burlison claims the alleged Legacy Program possesses “physics we have never seen.” On March 22, the State of the World Forum is planning to hold the first AI × NHI Convergence Summit , reflecting a growing sense that artificial and non-human intelligences are on a collision course. Steven Spielberg says his film “Disclosure Day” stems from a “sneaking suspicion that we are not alone here on Earth right now.” Recursive self-improvement was supposed to be our trick, not someone else’s. (Disclosure: I have a financial interest in PSI .) Thanks for reading The Innermost Loop! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.
